channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: DOJ lawyer who criticized admin over Maryland man's deportation placed on leave
item.description: Erez Reuveni, one of the DOJ's senior immigration lawyers, has been placed on leave after criticizing the Trump administration over mistakenly deporting a Maryland man to a prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stellantis joins Ford in offering employee discounts to public
item.description: The move follows in the steps of Ford Motor Company, which announced a similar bargain earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Parts of the South and Midwest, still reeling from violent storms, tornadoes and flooding that have killed more than a dozen people, face new flooding and tornado warnings that forecasters said could last for days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Actor Glen Powell launches organic condiments line
item.description: Actor and producer Glen Powell has launched Smash Kitchen, a line of organic condiments that includes non-GMO pantry essentials.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: Tariffs will make sneakers, jeans and almost everything Americans wear cost more, trade groups warn
item.description: Sending children back to school in new sneakers, jeans and T-shirts is likely to cost U.S. families significantly more this fall.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Clayton leads Florida to NCAA title game, scoring 34 points in 79-73 victory over SEC rival Auburn
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Walter Clayton Jr. is having an NCAA Tournament for the ages. Florida now just needs him to deliver one more time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Angry protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: St. Louis family's prank leads to lobster's prolonged life
item.description: What started off as an April Fool’s prank turned into something more than what Brea Stenzel, her family and a lobster were expecting.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: The Final Four again has multiple 5th-year starters. This time, it's a farewell to an uncommon era
item.description: “Credit to the NCAA for giving us a year back because that COVID year, that wasn’t real, honestly. Nah, it wasn’t fun at all.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Is anything collectible worth collecting? What experts say
item.description: You may have been promised as a child that collecting plush toys and waxy baseball cards would pay off in the future.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate passes budget, setting up showdown with House over Trump agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans voted early Saturday morning to pass a budget resolution that will be critical to advancing President Trump’s legislative agenda, but the measure breaks with House Republicans on several big issues, setting the stage for a showdown between the two chambers later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors seek 7 years in prison for disgraced ex-US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prosecutors are seeking more than seven years in prison for disgraced former U.S. Rep. George Santos after he pleaded guilty to federal fraud and identity theft charges. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Medicare won't cover anti-obesity GLP-1 drugs as Biden admin proposed
item.description: In November last year, the Biden administration proposed a new rule that would allow drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound to be covered by Medicare and Medicaid, expanding access for roughly 3.4 million Medicare users and about 4 million Medicaid enrollees.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LA County reaches $4 billion agreement to settle sexual abuse claims at juvenile facilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County has reached a $4 billion agreement to settle nearly 7,000 claims of sexual abuse in juvenile facilities since 1959, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anaheim mayor wants Los Angeles Angels to change their name
item.description: In an open-letter about the Angels future, the Anaheim mayor called for the team to be named for the city it actually represents.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Columbia must give 30 days' notice before sharing student records with Congress' antisemitism probe
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University must give detained activist Mahmoud Khalil and other students 30 days' notice before handing over any more documents to Congress as it investigates antisemitism on college campuses, a federal judge in New York ruled Friday. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illegal immigrant charged after Virginia man found stabbed to death in stranger's home: ICE
item.description: A Salvadoran illegal immigrant has been arrested and charged after allegedly stabbing a Virginia man to death in a stranger's home on March 27.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disgraced former Cardinal Theodore McCarrick has died at age 94
item.description: Theodore McCarrick, a once-powerful Catholic cardinal who was defrocked by Pope Francis in 2019 after a Vatican investigation determined he had molested adults and children, has died. He was 94.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Viral video shows Florida charter captain arguing with boater; police investigating
item.description: A viral video showing a fight between two Florida boaters has sparked both a police investigation and strong reactions from people online.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Dow plunges past 2,000 in a worsening global sell-off after China retaliates against Trump tariffs
item.description: The worldwide sell-off for financial markets is slamming into an even higher, scarier gear. The S&P 500 tumbled 5.7% Friday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average plunged 2,100 points and the Nasdaq composite dropped 5.7%.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bondi announces charges in MS-13 killing as the Trump administration highlights its gang crackdown
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Three alleged MS-13 gang members have been federally charged in connection with a killing a decade ago in Florida, U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi announced Friday, seeking to highlight the Trump administration's push to prosecute violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas Pete hot sauces recalled in 10 states
item.description: Two varieties of Texas Pete hot sauces have been recalled in 10 states over issues with bottling and labeling.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, NewsNation

item.title: HHS staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to give Congress more power over tariffs
item.description: If passed, the Trade Review Act of 2025 would require the president to notify lawmakers of an imposition or increase in tariffs within 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: How the White House calculated reciprocal tariffs
item.description: President Trump triumphantly held up a poster board in the Rose Garden on Wednesday showing the reciprocal tariffs he plans to impose on nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No more cheap skirts: Trump ends tax exemption for low-value Chinese imports
item.description: A notice to customers dazzled by the low-priced products on Chinese shopping apps: the days of getting trendy clothing, tools and gag gifts that cost less than lunch delivered to your door in 10 days are probably numbered.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Some Republicans vote to reject President Trump’s Canada tariffs
item.description: On Wednesday, a group of Republican senators voted along with Democrats on a resolution to undo President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on Canadian imports.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Bodega cats make New Yorkers' hearts purr, even if they violate state regulations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City’s “bodega cats” are beloved fixtures in the Big Apple — but they’re on the wrong side of the law.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 6 people are injured after a truck crashed into a building and pedestrians in Boston
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A box truck crashed into a building Tuesday afternoon in Boston’s Chinatown neighborhood and hit several pedestrians before flipping onto its side, city officials said. The driver and five others were injured in what authorities said appears to have been an accident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
